title: Local Networking, Name Resolution, and Secure Remote Access · Terminal Checkpoint
summary: Restore access to a host by testing local configuration, route, name resolution, listening sockets, and SSH trust in causal order. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a layered connectivity and SSH trust runbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Interpret interface addresses, routes, neighbors, and listening or established sockets as different network evidence.
Trace hosts files, resolver configuration, search domains, DNS queries, caches, and application lookup results.
Test local process, local socket, route, name resolution, transport, and application protocol with the smallest useful probe.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Host has 192.0.2.40/24, default route 192.0.2.1, DNS 192.0.2.53, and service port 443. Name lookup succeeds while the socket is absent; test layers and preserve SSH host-key trust. Competency probe: Use scp, sftp, rsync, and SSH forwarding with explicit direction, permissions, resume, exposure, and verification.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use scp, sftp, rsync, and ssh forwarding with explicit direction, permissions, resume, exposure, and verification.

content.text: Host has 192.0.2.40/24, default route 192.0.2.1, DNS 192.0.2.53, and service port 443. Name lookup succeeds while the socket is absent; test layers and preserve SSH host-key trust. Competency probe: Use scp, sftp, rsync, and SSH forwarding with explicit direction, permissions, resume, exposure, and verification.

content.title: Fault-isolation decision
content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the Remote Copy Tunnel symptom in a layered connectivity and SSH trust runbook during this quiz case.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: GNU Coreutils Manual — Controls file, text, identity, process, filesystem, and common utility behavior.

hints: Name the Remote Copy Tunnel precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Host has 192.0.2.40/24, default route 192.0.2.1, DNS 192.0.2.53, and service port 443. Name lookup succeeds while the socket is absent; test layers and preserve SSH host-key trust. Competency probe: Use scp, sftp, rsync, and SSH forwarding with explicit direction, permissions, resume, exposure, and verification.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: SSH port forwarding exposes only harmless local traffic by definition.

stimulus.title: Remote Copy Tunnel failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: SSH port forwarding exposes only harmless local traffic by definition.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use scp, sftp, rsync, and SSH forwarding with explicit direction, permissions, resume, exposure, and verification.

options.text: Run the Remote Copy Tunnel boundary case for a layered connectivity and SSH trust runbook, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.
Accept the Remote Copy Tunnel result because visible a layered connectivity and SSH trust runbook output is the highest authority. This does not resolve diagnose the hidden failure.

For diagnose the hidden failure, apply Remote Copy Tunnel without its stated preconditions, omit the a layered connectivity and SSH trust runbook trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Remote Copy Tunnel for a layered connectivity and SSH trust runbook.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Remote Copy Tunnel: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Host has 192.0.2.40/24, default route 192.0.2.1, DNS 192.0.2.53, and service port 443. Name lookup succeeds while the socket is absent; test layers and preserve SSH host-key trust. Competency probe: Use scp, sftp, rsync, and SSH forwarding with explicit direction, permissions, resume, exposure, and verification.
steps.why: Retrieving Remote Copy Tunnel through the concrete a layered connectivity and SSH trust runbook case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use scp, sftp, rsync, and SSH forwarding with explicit direction, permissions, resume, exposure, and verification.
